findings.problem_statement: Charges adapter helpers that accept a raw *entdb.Client can silently bypass the transaction already carried in ctx, producing partial writes during charge advancement.
evidence: AGENTS.md: 'In openmeter/billing/charges/.../adapter, keep Ent access transaction-aware even in shared helper functions. If a helper accepts a raw *entdb.Client, still wrap its body with entutils.TransactingRepo(...) / TransactingRepoWithNoValue(...) so it rebinds to the transaction already carried in ctx.'
openmeter/billing/charges/adapter.go defines the adapter surface for charges lifecycle.
openmeter/billing/charges/service.go exposes Create / AdvanceCharges / ApplyPatches which drive multi-step writes.
findings.root_cause: Ent transactions are propagated implicitly through ctx; a helper that receives a *entdb.Client but does not call entutils.TransactingRepo will use the non-tx client and escape the outer transaction.
fix_direction: Audit every helper under openmeter/billing/charges/**/adapter that takes *entdb.Client.
Wrap each helper body with entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue so it rebinds to any tx in ctx.
Add a lint/review checklist item requiring tx-aware wrapping for new charge adapter helpers.

findings.problem_statement: Credits-disabled deployments can still perform real ledger writes if any of the independent wiring layers forgets to guard on credits.enabled.
evidence: AGENTS.md: 'credits.enabled needs explicit guarding at multiple layers: ledger-backed customer credit handlers in api/v3/server, customer ledger hooks, and namespace/default-account provisioning are wired separately and must each stay disabled when credits are off.'

AGENTS.md: 'When credits.enabled is false, app/common wires ledger account services/resolvers to noop implementations. Any ledger account backfill that must write real ledger_accounts / ledger_customer_accounts rows needs to construct concrete ledger account + resolver adapters directly.'
app/common/customer.go wires customer service with ledger and subject hooks.
openmeter/ledger/customerbalance is exposed to api/v3/server credits endpoints.
findings.root_cause: The credits feature is cross-cutting across unrelated call graphs (HTTP handlers, customer hooks, namespace provisioning), so a single guard cannot stop ledger side effects originating elsewhere.
fix_direction: Map every call site that writes to ledger_accounts or ledger_customer_accounts.
For each, verify the Wire provider falls back to a noop when credits.enabled is false.
Add an integration test that boots with credits.enabled=false and asserts zero rows are written to ledger_* tables under representative flows.

findings.problem_statement: Notification event payload versioning is implicit; hardcoded version constants in the notification pipeline create silent compatibility risk across producers (billing-worker, balance-worker, ingest) and the Svix-backed consumer.
evidence: openmeter/notification/consumer/consumer.go subscribes to system events and dispatches to Svix; the producers emit from openmeter/billing/worker and openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker.
AGENTS.md references a /notification skill covering 'payload versioning'.
openmeter/watermill/eventbus/eventbus.go routes typed events; version is carried in the payload rather than the topic.
findings.root_cause: Payload schema version is encoded as constants inside emitter packages rather than being derived from the TypeSpec/OpenAPI contract, so a producer can silently emit a newer shape than the consumer expects.
fix_direction: Catalog every payload version constant in openmeter/notification and its producers.
Add an explicit version handshake (or contract test) between producer and consumer packages.
Pin webhook payload versions to TypeSpec models where possible so SDK consumers receive a stable shape.

findings.problem_statement: Sequential timestamped Atlas migrations plus atlas.sum chain hashing produces predictable merge conflicts on long-running feature branches.
evidence: AGENTS.md documents the migration flow: 'atlas migrate --env local diff <migration-name>' writes to tools/migrate/migrations/ and updates atlas.sum.
AGENTS.md references a /rebase skill that handles 'sequential migrations, atlas.sum conflicts, Ent regeneration'.
atlas.hcl pins schema source to ent://openmeter/ent/schema and migrations dir to file://tools/migrate/migrations.
findings.root_cause: Atlas chain hash in atlas.sum enforces a linear migration history; any two branches that both add a migration will collide by construction.
fix_direction: When rebasing, delete the branch's migration + its atlas.sum entry, rebase, and regenerate with atlas migrate diff.
Regenerate Ent code (make generate) before the atlas diff so the schema tree matches.
Use the /rebase skill checklist to keep atlas.sum integrity.

findings.problem_statement: context.Background() / context.TODO() introductions in application code silently drop cancellation, deadlines, and request-scoped values, violating the project rule.
evidence: AGENTS.md: 'Do not introduce context.Background() or context.TODO() to sidestep missing context propagation in application code. Either propagate the caller's context through the full call path, or remove the unused context.Context parameter.'
AGENTS.md: 'In tests, prefer t.Context() when a testing.T or testing.TB is available instead of introducing context.Background().'
findings.root_cause: When a helper doesn't receive a ctx, engineers frequently reach for context.Background() rather than plumbing the caller's ctx through, which breaks tracing and cancellation across Kafka consumers and HTTP handlers.
fix_direction: Grep for context.Background()/context.TODO() in openmeter/ and app/common/.
Plumb the caller's ctx through every call site; if ctx is truly unused, remove the parameter.
In tests, replace context.Background() with t.Context().
